name: Eugene Wright
abstract: Eugene Joseph Wright (May 29, 1923 – December 30, 2020) was an American jazz bassist who was a member of the Dave Brubeck Quartet.

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: Eugene Wright was the bass player, and all the guys were just fantastic players. And we did more rehearsing than playing but we just loved getting together every week just to rehearse.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: Eugene Wright was so wonderful all those years. ... Paul, and at times Gene, did play with me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] So that's Leon White, trombone, and Bill Perkins, guitar, and Eugene Wright on piano and you on saxophone... [INTERVIEWEE] Raby. I think, Raby, drummer.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Holloway
about_quotes.evidence: when I first really started making some what I call “serious money” I was working with Eugene Wright, I don’t know if you’re familiar with him, he was a bass player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Red Holloway interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Militello
about_quotes.evidence: when I go to do the audition they were preparing for a gig they had to do in New York and who is there is Joe Morello and Eugene Wright.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Militello interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 14, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Preston Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: There was Eugene Wright, Eugene Wright and his Dukes of Swing... So I stayed with them fellas.
